tasks_completed.changes: Added `.codex` line above existing `.codex/` entry; existing entry only matched directories, not the tracked empty sentinel file
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: .gitignore had `.codex/` (directory pattern) but the actual tracked file `.codex` is a bare file, so the pattern did not match it. Adding `.codex` (no trailing slash) covers the file.

tasks_completed.description: Ran git rm --cached .codex to remove .codex from git index
tasks_completed.changes: git rm --cached .codex — file removed from index, remains on disk
tasks_completed.rationale: File was already tracked before .gitignore rule existed. git rm --cached untracked it without disk deletion.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — file committed before .gitignore pattern existed
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: .gitignore had `.codex/` (directory-only pattern) but .codex is an empty file. Pattern mismatch left the file tracked.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Added bare `.codex` entry to .gitignore. git rm --cached removed it from the index. Both entry types (.codex and .codex/) now present, covering file and directory cases.

self_verification.notes: git ls-files .codex returns empty (untracked). git check-ignore -v .codex returns `.gitignore:12:.codex	.codex` confirming the new bare entry matches. git diff shows exactly 1 line added. No commit made per instructions.

dev.qa_notes: Verify: (1) git ls-files .codex in /root/orchestra returns empty; (2) git check-ignore .codex returns exit 0; (3) .codex file still exists on disk at /root/orchestra/.codex
